chunks.text: IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, BENCH AT SUKKUR Const. Petition No. D-819 of 2023 (Sardar Ali Jarwar & others v. P.O. Sindh & others) Present:- Mr. Justice Muhammad Iqbal Kalhoro & Mr. Justice Arbab Ali Hakro Mr. Rahmat Ali alias Raza Ali, Advocate for petitioners. Mr. Zulfiqar Ali Naich, Assistant A.G-Sindh. Date of Hearing & Order: 13-02-2024 O R D E R MUHAMMAD IQBAL KALHORO, J:- Petitioners had applied for the post of Field Assistant (BPS-11) in the department of Agriculture Extension in the recruitment process held in the year 2008. Although they qualified the test, but did not come on merits, therefore, were not appointed. They have filed this petition and have referred to an order dated 08.03.2023, passed by this Court in number of petitions including C.P.No.D-1234 of 2011, whereby case of those petitioners, identically placed, was referred to the Grievance Redressal Committee (GRC) for a decision. 2. Learned counsel for petitioners submits that their case may also be referred to the same Committee. Learned AAG, however, on the contrary has submitted that none of the petitioners whose case was referred to GRC has succeeded and their case was dismissed on the ground that they had obtained less marks than the last one who had obtained 83-marks and was appointed. 3. Admittedly, petitioners had obtained less marks than 83- marks, the marks obtained by last candidate. More so, this petition suffers from latches. The recruitment process held in the year 2008 has been questioned. Therefore, we do not find any merit in this petition and dismiss it accordingly. JUDGE JUDGE Ahmad
